titleStmt.title: Letter from Margaret Huxley to Thomas Kirkpatrick, 3 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Margaret Huxley

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Margaret Huxley (1856-1940) to Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) requesting use of the facilities of the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) for exam purposes. According to Huxley the rooms at the Dublin Metropolitan Technical School for nurses are overcrowded and inconvenient for students. In 1915 Margaret Huxley took over the position as matron of the Dublin University Women's Voluntary Aid Detachment's Hospital. The VAD was a section of the Red Cross used to train civilian nurses to provide medical assistance during the war. The following year Huxley was tending to casualties of the Easter Rising as she opened her own private nursing home on Lower Mount Street.From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Elpis, Lower Mount Street, Dublin, Ireland
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ELPIS, L.R MOUNT S.T, DUBLIN. Feb. 3rd 1916 Dear Dr Kirkpatrick At the examinations
held by the Dublin
Metropolitan Technical
School for Nurses the
Pupils are overcrowded
& greatly inconvenienced
for want of space in
the Nurses' Hostel.
The College of Physicians
have splendid rooms.
I wonder whether
the Authorities would
lend them to us
for two consecutive
evenings in March
when our next exam
takes place. If you would
kindly make known
our need perhaps the
College would consider
the matter favourably
for us. On former
occasions they have
been so very kind
to nurses that I ven
âture to ask their
help again. Thanking you
in anticipation. I am
Yrs truly. M. Huxley
Hon. Sec. D.M.J.S for
Nurses
